patterns.summary: Performs best in the morning (avg 0.86) vs afternoon (avg 0.54).
patterns.detail: Across 8 sessions, morning focus consistently outscored afternoon by ~0.3. Schedule the hardest work before noon.

patterns.summary: Follow-ups are completed late 80% of the time.
patterns.detail: 4 of 5 follow-ups slipped past their due date — a recurring drop-off after the first touch.

bottlenecks.description: Follow-ups consistently slip past their due date.
bottlenecks.impact: Warm opportunities go cold; revenue left on the table.

recommended_automations.title: Auto follow-up reminders
recommended_automations.what: Automatically schedule 24h/3d/7d follow-up nudges until a reply lands.
recommended_automations.explanation: Follow-ups slip 80% of the time; an automatic cadence removes the manual step that keeps getting dropped.

recommended_agents.purpose: Draft and track follow-ups so threads never go cold
recommended_agents.explanation: The late-follow-up pattern is consistent enough to justify a dedicated agent that prepares the follow-up for approval.

workflow_improvements.title: Protect mornings for deep work
workflow_improvements.change: Block 9-11am for the hardest task; move admin and low-stakes calls to the afternoon.
workflow_improvements.explanation: Morning performance averages 0.86 vs 0.54 in the afternoon — align your hardest work with your peak.

summary: Two clear patterns: strong mornings and slipping follow-ups. The biggest lever is automating follow-up cadence. Recommendations only — nothing was changed.
